statusLine: EU fuel security is under pressure with 15 of 27 countries showing critical levels in at least one fuel type, despite average reserves remaining above mandatory minimums.
fullAnalysis:
European fuel security shows concerning disparities despite adequate EU-wide averages. While the EU maintains 94.5 days of petrol, 95.8 days of diesel, and 89.9 days of jet fuel reserves on average — all above the 90-day mandatory minimum — the distribution reveals significant vulnerabilities. Romania faces the most severe situation with all three fuel types in critical status, including just 20.2 days of diesel reserves.

The data shows 15 of 27 EU countries have critical reserves in at least one fuel category. Diesel appears particularly strained, with 14 countries below the 90-day threshold. Jet fuel shows the tightest supplies overall, averaging 89.9 days and affecting major economies including Germany (61.4 days) and France (59.6 days). Poland and Spain, both significant consumers, show critical levels across all fuel types.

Brent crude oil has declined 13.77% to $94.22 per barrel, potentially providing some relief from recent price pressures. However, with crude still elevated approximately 27% since the Middle East conflict began, fuel prices remain elevated at €1.789/L for petrol and €2.00/L for diesel. The recent strategic reserve releases following Middle East supply disruptions help explain current stock pressures, particularly in countries showing critical levels.
keyPoints: 15 of 27 EU countries show critical fuel reserves in at least one category, despite EU averages above 90-day minimums
Romania faces the most severe shortage with all fuels critical, including just 20.2 days of diesel reserves
Diesel supplies are most strained with 14 countries below the 90-day mandatory threshold
Jet fuel averages only 89.9 days EU-wide, just below the safety threshold, affecting major economies
Recent strategic reserve releases due to Middle East disruptions contribute to current stock pressures
